title: Chinese scientists build silver-free heterojunction solar cell with 25.2% efficiency
content: The research team developed a plasma interface engineering method to improve indium tin oxide layers, solving adhesion, contact resistance, and stability issues for copper electroplating. The optimized process enabled uniform copper metallization and boosted device efficiency to 25.2%, significantly outperforming untreated reference cells.

title: Two-year testing shows how PV plants increase local temperatures in semi-arid regions
content: A two-year field study in a 100 MW photovoltaic plant in semi-arid Inner Mongolia combined ground-based sensors, radiation measurements, and UAV thermal imaging to quantify how large-scale PV installations alter local air temperature, surface temperature, and energy balance compared with nearby non-PV areas. Results show consistent site-scale warming of 0.8 C.

title: New Zealand’s grid operator finds emerging energy gap in early 2030s
content: Transpower's latest assessment of New Zealand's balance between electricity supply and demand suggests an energy gap could emerge in the early 2030s, even if all committed and likely investments are delivered without delay.

title: Chinese solar exports surge 125% in March on policy change rush, not underlying demand acceleration
content: The Chinese customs data show a March surge as buyers rushed shipments ahead of April’s tax-rebate change.

title: Pakistan plans to scrap licensing fee for PV systems up to 25 kW
content: The move seeks to reverse aspects of the Nepra Prosumers Regulations 2026, which centralized approvals and is creating delays and financial barriers for rooftop solar adoption.

title: Key takeaways from Austria’s Solar Solutions
content: Around 100 exhibitors presented their products at the Vienna trade fair. Visitor numbers were limited, partly because the event coincided with the launch of the first funding call for photovoltaic systems and storage this year.

title: Record solar, wind surge drives 3.3% drop in India’s fossil generation in 2025
content: India’s renewable power generation from solar, wind, hydro and bioenergy rose by a record 98 TWh (+24%) in 2025, driven by strong growth in solar and wind, according to Ember. The increase was twice the country’s electricity demand growth of 49 TWh, which was relatively low due to milder temperatures and slower industrial activity. As a result, India’s fossil power generation fell by 3.3%.

title: U.S. government sets preliminary anti-dumping duties on solar imports from India, Indonesia, and Laos
content: The U.S. Department of Commerce has issued preliminary anti-dumping determinations for three key solar-exporting nations, establishing duties of up to 123% on crystalline silicon photovoltaic cells and modules.

title: Australia’s energy commission targets lower solar curtailment
content: Australia's Energy Market Commission says introducing a new distribution planning framework and undertaking enhanced data reporting will help to reduce rooftop solar curtailment.

title: Wind co-location: TotalEnergies takes FID on 1GW Kazakhstan project, Downing develops in Finland
content: TotalEnergies is pushing ahead with a co-located battery storage and gigawatt-scale wind project in Kazakhstan, Central Asia.

title: Australian court amends consent for Hydrostor’s 1.6GWh long-duration energy storage project following appeal
content: The Land and Environment Court of New South Wales has upheld an appeal regarding Hydrostor’s 1.6GWh A-CAES project.
